exits.label: ◂ off the pilgrim road — back to the Grey Wayshrine

npcs.name: Brother Calwin
npcs.title: newly set out, full of certainty
npcs.hail: *(a soul stands at the first milestone with its face turned up the road toward the distant grey shrine, lit with the particular radiance of a pilgrim newly set out — all the doubt still ahead of it, all the certainty still intact — and it grips a walking-staff it does not need, for the dead do not tire, with the fervent grip of a soul that has decided the walking itself is the point and has not yet walked far enough to test the belief)*

npcs.name: Goodwife Tamsin
npcs.title: halfway, and wavering
npcs.hail: *(a soul sits on a cairn at the road's midpoint, neither climbing nor descending, with the particular exhaustion of a pilgrim that has walked exactly far enough to lose the certainty that started it and not yet far enough to find whatever replaces it — staring back down the road it came and up the road it has left, and unable, for a long frozen while now, to choose which way to face)*

npcs.name: Old Ferd
npcs.title: arrived, and will not go in
npcs.hail: *(a soul stands at the last milestone, an arm's length from the grey shrine that is the road's whole end, and does not take the final step — has not, you sense, taken it in a very long time — for it walked the entire long road to reach the destination and discovered, at the threshold of it, that arriving was the one thing it did not actually want, the walking having become the only self it had)*

variants.text: *(It turns to you, radiant, the way only the newly-set-out can be, and lifts its staff in greeting.)* …A *fellow* traveler on the *Long* *Walk!* *(it beams)* Or — *(it studies your cold)* — no. Not a *pilgrim.* *Colder* than that. *(undeterred)* No matter! *All* feet are *welcome* on the *road* to the *Court.* *(it gestures up the long grey slope toward the distant shrine)* Do you *know* of it, friend? The *Long* *Walk* — the *old* *devotion* — the souls who *will* not be *ferried* to *Kelemvor's* *Court* like *cargo*, but *walk* it, on *foot*, *every* *grey* *mile*, as a — as a *penance*, a *prayer*, a *proof* of *earnestness.* *(its face shines)* I *set* out *three* *days* *past.* My *feet* — well, the *dead* have *no* *feet* to *ache*, which is *almost* a *pity*, the *ache* would *mean* something — but my *SOUL*, friend, my *soul* has *never* felt so — so *aimed.* *(it grips the staff)* *Every* *step* *toward* the *Judge* is a *step* *worth* *taking.* That is the *whole* of my *creed*, and I have *three* *days*' *certainty* in it, and I would not *trade* that certainty for *anything* the *road* *ahead* might *offer.* *(a bright, untested smile)* …*Walk* with me a while? I do *love* to *speak* the *creed* *aloud.* It *firms* the *footing.*

nodes.speaker: Brother Calwin
nodes.text: *(It leans on its staff, eyes shining up the road, brimming with the eagerness of a soul that has not yet been walking long enough to doubt.)* Ask, friend! *(it beams)* Or *better* — *affirm.* I find the *road* goes *easier* with a *voice* beside it. But I'll take *questions*, if questions are what you've *got.* *Nothing* you ask can *shake* a *creed* *three* *days* *strong.*

choices.text: "Why walk it, Calwin? The dead get ferried to the Court for free. You're choosing the hard road on purpose. What does the walking buy that the ferry doesn't?"

"There's a soul halfway up the road who's stopped — can't choose whether to go on or turn back. And one at the very top who won't take the last step. Have you seen where this creed leads, three days on?"

*(the one who's already been to the end of the road)* "I'll tell you something no pilgrim on this road knows, Calwin, because I've done the one thing the Long Walk is the long way round to: I've *been* to the Court. Stood before Kelemvor himself. So before you walk three hundred grey miles on a creed, hear it from a soul that's seen the destination — and then decide if the walking's still the point."
"Hold to your creed, Calwin. Three days' certainty is worth having. Walk well."

nodes.speaker: Brother Calwin
nodes.text: *(It lights up, delighted to expound.)* …*Why* *walk* it. *(it plants the staff)* Because the *ferry*, friend — the *boatman*, the *mist*, the *easy* *crossing* — it *delivers* you to the *Judge* *unchanged.* The *same* soul that *died*, *set* *down* at the *Court* like a *parcel*, having *risked* *nothing*, *proven* *nothing*, *earned* *nothing.* *(its face shines)* But the *Long* *Walk* — *every* *grey* *mile* is a *mile* the soul *chooses* the *Judge* *again*, and *again*, and *again.* By the *time* a *pilgrim* *reaches* the *Court*, it is not the *soul* that *died* — it is a *soul* that has *walked* *three* *hundred* *miles* of *grey* *devotion* to *get* there, and *that* soul *arrives* *transformed*, *worthy*, *ready* to be *judged.* *(it grips the staff, fervent)* The *walking* is not the *means* to the *Court*, friend. The *walking* *IS* the *judgment* — a *judgment* you *pass* on *yourself*, *step* by *step*, *proving* with your *own* *feet* that you *meant* it. *(a radiant, untested smile)* *That* is what the *walking* *buys* that the *ferry* *cannot*: it buys you a *self* you'd be *proud* to *hand* the *Judge.* *(softly, certain)* I have *three* *days* of that *self* *already.* Imagine the *self* I'll be at *three* *hundred* *miles.*

nodes.speaker: Brother Calwin
nodes.text: *(Its radiant certainty flickers — just for an instant — and then reasserts itself, a touch too firmly.)* …*Stopped.* *(it glances up the road, where the doubter sits on her cairn)* *Halfway.* *(and at the top, the one who won't go in)* …*Arrived* and *won't* — *(it grips the staff harder)* …*No.* *Those* are souls of *weaker* *creed* than *mine*, friend. *(but the certainty has a hairline now)* The *one* who *stopped* — she *lost* her *reason*, *clearly*, let the *road* *wear* her *certainty* *thin.* And the *one* at the *top* — *(it falters)* — *arrived*, and *won't* take the *last* *step* — that's — that's just — *(it cannot quite explain it)* …*every* *devotion* has its *failures*, friend. Its *fallen.* That *proves* *nothing* about the *creed* — only about the *creed* of *those* *who* *fell.* *(it turns its face back up the road, deliberately)* *I* will *not* *stop* *halfway.* *I* will *not* *balk* at the *top.* *My* *creed* is — *(a flicker of the first real doubt)* …*is* it *different*, though? From *theirs*, three days *in?* *(it shakes it off)* *No.* I'll not *let* you *plant* the *seed*, cold one. I've *three* *days*' *certainty* and I *mean* to *keep* it. *(but it glances, once more, uneasily, at the stopped soul halfway up)* …*Mean* to.

nodes.speaker: Brother Calwin
nodes.text: *(The radiance falters into something between hunger and dread — for a pilgrim, news from the destination is the one thing both longed-for and feared.)* …You've — you've *BEEN.* *(barely)* To the *Court.* *Stood* before — *(it grips the staff white-knuckled)* …*tell* me, then. *(a whisper)* Is it — is the *Judge* *worthy* of the *walking?* Is *Kelemvor* what the *creed* *says* — *stern* and *just* and *waiting* at the *end* of the *grey* *miles* to *weigh* the *self* I'll have *made* of *myself?* *(it searches your cold face)* …Or are you about to *tell* me something that *un-makes* the *road.* *(its voice shakes)* Because I can *see* it on you, cold one — you've *something* to *say* that you're not *sure* I should *hear* — and I am *three* *days* *into* a *devotion* I have *staked* my *whole* *after* on, and you are about to either *confirm* it or *shatter* it, and I — *(it draws a breath it doesn't need)* …*say* it. *Whatever* it *is.* A *creed* that *can't* *survive* the *truth* about its *own* *destination* isn't *worth* the *walking.* If you've *seen* the *Court* — *tell* me *true* what's *there.* I'd *rather* *break* on a *true* *thing* than *arrive*, *three* *hundred* *miles* *on*, at a *lie.*

nodes.speaker: Brother Calwin
nodes.text: *(It waits, staff gripped, three days' certainty trembling in the balance — the destination, named at last by a soul that has seen it.)* …*Tell* me, cold one. What's at the *end* of the *Long* *Walk?*

choices.text: "The Judge is real, Calwin — and worthier and sadder than your creed knows. Kelemvor is a tired god bound to a Wall he hates, who's forbidden to even witness the souls who need it most. He doesn't want your three hundred miles of penance. He wants to be *relieved.* So walk if the walking makes you, but don't walk to *earn* him — he's not weighing your worthiness. He's drowning in his own. Walk to *arrive* and *help*, not to *prove.*"

"Here's the truth that un-makes the road, Calwin, and I'll not soften it: the walking doesn't change what the Judge sees. He weighs what you *did* in life, not how earnestly you *traveled* after. Three hundred miles of penance buys you nothing at his scale — it only buys *you* a self you can live with on the road. Which is real, and worth something. Just not what you think. The creed's half-true: the walking transforms the walker. It does nothing to the Judge."

nodes.speaker: Brother Calwin
nodes.text: *(It is silent a long, long moment, the staff loose in its grip — and then, slowly, something in its face that was *fervor* becomes something quieter and truer: purpose without the certainty.)* …He wants to be *relieved.* *(barely)* The *Judge* I've been *walking* to *earn* — he's not *weighing* me. He's *drowning.* *(it looks up the long grey road, and then back at you)* …Then I've had it *backwards*, haven't I. *Three* *days* — *gods*, only *three*, *imagine* if I'd *walked* *three* *hundred* *miles* to *PROVE* myself to a *god* who *needs* *PROVING* to *least* of *anyone.* *(it almost laughs, shaken)* You haven't *un-made* the *road*, cold one. You've — you've *re-aimed* it. *(it straightens, and the radiance returns, but changed — humbler, harder, truer)* I'll *still* *walk.* But not to *earn* him. To *reach* him — and to *arrive* as a soul that came to *help* *carry* the *thing* he's *drowning* in, not to *add* my *worthiness* to his *pile.* *(fierce, quiet)* *That's* a *creed* worth the *miles.* Not *'every* step *proves* me.'* *'Every* step *brings* me *closer* to a *god* who *needs* *hands*, not *worshippers.'* *(it grips your cold hand)* *Thank* you, cold one. You've *saved* me *two* *hundred* and *ninety-seven* *miles* of *walking* in *exactly* the *wrong* *direction* of *spirit.* I'll *walk* the *same* *road* now — but I'll *arrive* *useful* instead of *arriving* *proud.* *That's* the *one* *transformation* the *road* was *really* *for.*

nodes.speaker: Brother Calwin
nodes.text: *(The radiance drains, and for a moment it is just a soul that has been told the thing it walked three days to avoid hearing — and then, slowly, it finds something steadier underneath the lost certainty.)* …The *walking* does *nothing* to the *Judge.* *(barely)* It only *changes* the *walker.* *(it sinks down onto the milestone)* …*Half-true.* *(a long breath)* That's — that's *worse* than a *lie*, cold one, a *half-truth*, because you *can't* *fully* *throw* it *away.* *(it stares up the road)* I *staked* my *after* on *'the* walking *earns* the *Judge'* — and you're *telling* me the *earning* *part* is *empty*, and only the *walking-changes-the-walker* part is *real.* *(it is quiet a moment)* …So the *creed* is *vanity*, dressed as *devotion.* I've been *walking* to *impress* a *god* who *isn't* *looking* — and the *only* *real* *thing* the *road* *does* is *make* *me* *feel* *worthy*, which is — *(it winces)* — which is just *pride* with *blisters*, isn't it. *(it looks at you, disillusioned but not destroyed)* …*Thank* you for the *truth*, cold one. It *cost* me my *creed.* But a *creed* that *can't* *survive* being *true* was — *(it stands, slower, sadder, wiser)* …I don't *know* if I'll *keep* *walking*, now. Maybe I'll *take* the *ferry* like *cargo* after *all*, and *save* the *miles*, and *meet* the *Judge* as the *plain* *un-transformed* soul I *am.* *(quietly)* *That* might be the *humbler* *pilgrimage* — to *arrive* *admitting* you *earned* *nothing.* *(a thin, rueful smile)* You've *given* me a *harder* *road*, cold one, by *taking* the *easy* *certainty* *off* it. I'm not sure I *thank* you. But I think I *should.*

nodes.speaker: Brother Calwin
nodes.text: *(It beams, restored to full radiance by your blessing, and lifts its staff up the road.)* …*Walk* *well!* *(joyful)* And — *(it calls after you)* — if your *cold* *feet* ever *find* the *creed*, friend, the *road's* *always* *here!* It *climbs* the *same* *grey* *slope* it *climbed* *yesterday* and will *climb* *tomorrow*, and *every* *milestone's* a *prayer*, and *every* *prayer's* a *step*, and — *(it sets off, fervent and untested)* — *gods*, I do *love* the *certainty* of it. *Three* *days* and not a *crack* in it! *(it climbs, radiant, toward a road it has not yet walked far enough to doubt)* …*Mind* the *milestones*, cold one! They *count* the *prayers!* And the *prayers* — *(fainter, climbing)* — the *prayers* are the *POINT!* *(it does not look back at the soul stopped halfway, or the soul balked at the top, and you wonder how many days it has before it does.)*

variants.text: *(It does not get up from the cairn it sits on, only turns its tired face toward you — a soul caught exactly between two directions.)* …Don't tell me. *(flatly)* You're going to ask which way I'm *going.* *Up* or *down*, *on* or *back.* *(a hollow laugh)* *Everyone* asks. The *eager* ones, climbing up — they ask it *brightly*, *'which* way, *sister?', certain* I'll say *up.* And I — *(it looks up the road, then down)* …I've been *sitting* on this *cairn*, cold one, for — I've *lost* the *count.* A *long* *while.* *Halfway.* *(barely)* I set out like *that* one down there — *(it nods toward the radiant starter)* — *certain* the *walking* was the *point*, *certain* *every* *step* *earned* me *something.* And I *walked*, and I *walked*, and somewhere — *somewhere* around *here*, the *halfway* — the *certainty* just… *ran* *out.* *(it spreads tired hands)* And now I *can't* go *up*, because I no longer *believe* the *top* is *worth* it. And I can't go *down*, because *going* *back* means *admitting* the *miles* I *walked* were *wasted.* *(it meets your eyes)* So I *sit.* *Halfway.* *Facing* *neither* way. It's the *one* position that *doesn't* *require* me to *decide* whether the *whole* *thing* *meant* *anything.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Tamsin
nodes.text: *(It sits on the cairn, looking at nothing, a soul frozen between a road climbed and a road remaining.)* …*ask*, if you must. *(wearily)* But *mind* — I've *no* *answers.* That's *rather* the *whole* of my *trouble.* I *had* them, *certainties*, a *road's* *worth* — and I *spent* them, *climbing*, and *now* I've an *empty* *purse* and a *long* *way* *both* *directions.*

choices.text: "What ran out, Tamsin? You said the certainty just stopped, around the halfway. What did you stop believing?"

"The miles you walked aren't wasted just because you'd turn back, Tamsin. A road you walked is a road you *know* — that's not nothing, even if you don't reach the end. Going back isn't admitting waste. It's choosing what to do with what the walking taught you."

*(the one who's seen both ends of the road)* "You're stuck because you think the road has only two directions — finish or quit — and both feel like defeat. But I've been to the top, Tamsin, and I've been where the eager ones start, and here's the thing the road hides at its halfway: the destination was never the point, and neither was the starting. The *walking with others* was. You don't have to choose up or down. You could turn sideways — and walk the road as the one who *helps the next doubter sit down and find their feet.*"
"Take the time you need, Tamsin. The cairn'll hold you a while longer."

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Tamsin
nodes.text: *(It is quiet, dredging up the moment the road went hollow.)* …What I *stopped* *believing.* *(slowly)* It wasn't *one* *thing.* It was — *(it gestures up the road)* — I *believed*, *setting* *out*, that the *walking* would *change* me. *Transform* me. That I'd *arrive* at the *Court* a *better*, *worthier* *soul* than I *died.* *(it looks at its own hands)* And *here*, at the *halfway*, I — I *looked* at *myself*, cold one, *truly* *looked*, and I was — I was *exactly* the *same* *soul.* *(barely)* The *same* *fears*, the *same* *pettinesses*, the *same* *me*, *just* *footsore* in *spirit.* The *walking* *hadn't* *transformed* *anything.* It had just — *moved* the *same* *unchanged* *soul* a *hundred* *grey* *miles* *up* a *hill.* *(its voice cracks)* And I *thought* — *if* the *walking* *doesn't* *transform* me, then *reaching* the *top* *won't* either, and I'll *arrive* at the *Judge* the *same* *flawed* *thing* I *am* *now*, having *walked* a *hundred* *miles* to *change* *nothing.* *(it meets your eyes)* *That's* what *ran* out, cold one. Not my *faith* in the *Judge.* My *faith* that *I* could be *made* *better* by *effort.* And *once* *that* *went* — *(it spreads its hands)* — *what's* the *point* of *climbing* a *hill* to *meet* a *god* as the *same* *soul* you'd *have* *met* him *as* at the *bottom? Why* *walk*, if the *walking* *doesn't* *work?*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Tamsin
nodes.text: *(It looks at you, and something in the frozen exhaustion shifts, just slightly.)* …A *road* I *walked* is a *road* I *know.* *(slowly)* *Not* *waste*, just because I'd *turn* *back.* *(it considers it)* …You know, cold one, *nobody's* *said* that. The *eager* ones *say* *'don't* turn *back*, *sister*, *push* *on!'* — as if *turning* *back* were a *failure.* And *I've* *said* it *to* *myself*, a *hundred* *times*, *'if* you go *down* now the *miles* were *wasted', and* it's *kept* me *frozen* *here.* *(it stands, slowly, testing the thought)* But you're *saying* the *miles* *aren't* a *deposit* I *lose* if I don't *finish.* They're — they're a *thing* I *learned.* I *know* this *road* now. Every *milestone*, every *cairn*, the *exact* *place* the *certainty* *runs* *out.* *(it looks down the road, where the radiant starter climbs)* …I *know* *where* the *hollow* is. The *halfway.* The place that *stopped* *me.* *(quietly)* I could — I could *walk* *back* *down*, *couldn't* I, and *not* as a *failure*, but as a *soul* that *knows* the *road's* *worst* *mile* — and *warn* the *next* *eager* *climber* what's *coming*, so the *halfway* doesn't *freeze* *them* the way it *froze* *me.* *(a flicker of something that isn't quite hope but is its cousin)* That's — that's a *direction*, isn't it. *Not* up, *not* *back-in-defeat.* *Back-with-a-purpose.* *(it meets your eyes)* You've *not* *un-stuck* me, cold one. But you've *shown* me the *cairn* I'm *sitting* on *isn't* the *only* *place* a *halfway* *soul* can *be.* I'll — I'll *think* on *that.* It's *more* than I've *had* to *think* on in a *long* *frozen* *while.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Tamsin
nodes.text: *(It stares at you, and the frozen thing in it — the thing that has held it on this cairn for a long uncounted while — cracks all the way open.)* …*Sideways.* *(barely)* *Turn* — *sideways.* *(it stands, the exhaustion falling off it like a dropped pack)* *Neither* *up* *nor* *down.* I — cold one, I have *sat* on this *cairn* *certain* there were *only* *two* *ways* to *face*, and *both* of them *defeats*, and you've — you've just *told* me there's a *THIRD* *axis* I *never* — *(it laughs, the first real sound in a long while)* — *of* *COURSE.* The *road* *isn't* a *line* with a *win* at the *top* and a *quit* at the *bottom.* It's a *PLACE.* Full of *souls.* The *eager* ones *climbing*, the *balked* one at the *top*, the *next* *doubter* who'll *freeze* at *this* *exact* *cairn* a *week* from *now* — *(fierce, alight)* — and I *KNOW* this *halfway*, cold one. I *know* it *better* than *anyone* *alive* or *dead*, because I've *SAT* in it *longer* than *anyone.* *(it grips your cold hand)* I don't *have* to *choose* whether the *road* *meant* *anything.* I can *MAKE* it *mean* *something* — by *being* the *soul* who *sits* *with* the *next* *frozen* *pilgrim*, who *says* *'I* *know*, I *KNOW*, I've *been* on this *cairn* for *years*, and *here's* what I *learned*: the *walking* doesn't *transform* you, *and* *that's* *alright*, because the *POINT* was *never* the *transforming* — it was the *not* *walking* *ALONE.'* *(radiant, freed)* *That's* what I'll *do.* I'll *keep* the *halfway.* *Tend* it. Be the *soul* the *road's* *worst* *mile* *needs.* *(it looks up and down the long grey road, and for the first time it is not frozen — it is *home*)* You *didn't* *send* me *up* or *down*, cold one. You *gave* me the *one* *place* I was *already* *standing* and *made* it a *destination.* *Thank* you. The *halfway* has a *keeper* now. *Nobody* freezes *here* *alone* *again.*

nodes.speaker: Goodwife Tamsin
nodes.text: *(It nods, and settles back onto the cairn, but watches you go with less frozen eyes than before.)* …*Time.* *(a tired almost-smile)* Aye. The *cairn* will *hold* me. It's *held* me this *long.* *(it looks up the road, then down)* …You know the *strange* *thing*, cold one? *Everyone* who *passes* is in such a *HURRY.* The *eager* ones *rushing* *up*, *certain.* The *rare* *few* *hurrying* *back* *down*, *defeated.* And *me* — *(it spreads its hands)* — *sitting.* In the *one* *spot* *nobody* *wants* to *be.* *(quietly)* But it's *quiet* here, at the *halfway.* You can *see* the *whole* *road*, *both* *directions.* *Where* *everyone's* *been* and *where* *everyone's* *going.* *(a flicker)* *Maybe* that's *worth* *something*, the *seeing.* *Maybe* the *halfway* *isn't* a *failure* to *move.* *Maybe* it's the *one* *place* you can *see* the *whole* *thing* *clear.* *(it meets your eyes)* *Go* on, cold one. *Mind* the *milestones.* And if you *pass* the *halfway* *again* — *(a real, tired smile)* — I'll *likely* *still* be *here.* *Seeing.* It's *better* *company* than the *climbing*, *honest.* You've *time* to *talk*, when you've *stopped* *rushing* *somewhere.*

conversations.title: Old Ferd, Who Will Not Go In

variants.text: *(It stands at the last milestone, an arm's length from the grey shrine, its back to you, facing the destination it will not enter.)* …I *hear* you. *(it does not turn)* *Cold* feet on the *road.* *(quietly)* You've come a *long* *way*, if you've come *up* *here* — the *top*, the *end*, the *last* *milestone.* *Most* don't.* *Most* *freeze* at the *halfway*, like *poor* *Tamsin* down there. *(it almost laughs)* I *envy* her, some *days.* She *stopped* *before* she *learned* the *thing* I *learned* at the *TOP.* *(barely)* I *walked* it, cold one. The *whole* *Long* *Walk.* *Three* *hundred* *grey* *miles.* *Every* *milestone*, *every* *prayer*, *every* *step* a *step* *toward* the *Court* — and I *arrived*, *radiant*, *transformed*, *worthy*, *everything* the *creed* *promised* — and I *stood* *here*, *one* *step* *from* the *end*, and I — *(its voice cracks)* — I *couldn't* *go* *in.* *(it finally half-turns)* I *still* *can't.* It's been — I've *lost* the *count.* I just *stand* here. *Arrived* and *un-arrived* both. The *one* *pilgrim* who *walked* the *whole* *road* and *will* *not* *take* the *last* *step.* *(barely)* …Do you *know* *why*, cold one? Because I *worked* it *out*, *standing* *here*: the *walking* was the *only* *self* I *had.* And *going* *in* — *finishing* — means the *walking's* *over.* And a *pilgrim* with *no* *more* *road* is — is *nothing.* *Just* a *soul* at a *door.* So I — I *don't* *open* it. I *stay* a *pilgrim.* *Forever.* *One* *step* *short.*

nodes.speaker: Old Ferd
nodes.text: *(It stands at the threshold of the grey shrine, neither entering nor leaving, a soul that made it the whole way and stopped.)* …*ask*, cold one. *(quietly)* I've *time.* I've *nothing* *BUT* — that's the *whole* *trouble.* *(a hollow almost-laugh)* A *pilgrim* who *won't* *finish* his *pilgrimage* has *all* the *time* there *is*, and *no* *idea* what to *do* with it but *stand* here and *not* *open* a *door.*

choices.text: "You walked three hundred miles to reach this door, Ferd, and now you won't open it. What do you think is on the other side that's worth stopping an arm's length short of?"

"You said the walking was the only self you had. So you're not afraid of what's behind the door — you're afraid of being a soul with no more road. The pilgrimage became your whole identity, and finishing it means losing yourself. Is that it?"

*(the one who's been through the door he won't open)* "I'll tell you what's on the other side, Ferd — I've been through it. It's not an ending. It's the Court, and the Judge, and the work of the dead, which never finishes. You think going in means the road's over and you're nothing. But the door isn't the end of the walking. It's where the walking finally *arrives somewhere it can do some good.* You didn't walk three hundred miles to reach a door. You walked it to become someone worth what's past the door. You already are. Stop standing in your own doorway."
"Stand as long as you need, Ferd. The door'll wait."

nodes.speaker: Old Ferd
nodes.text: *(It looks at the grey shrine, the door it will not open, with a complicated dread.)* …What's *behind* it. *(quietly)* That's the *thing*, cold one — I'm *not* *afraid* of what's *behind* it. *(it almost laughs)* I *know* what's *behind* it. The *Court.* The *Judge.* *Judgment* — the *thing* I *walked* *three* *hundred* *miles* to *face*, *certain* I'd *face* it *worthy.* *(it spreads tired hands)* And I *AM* *worthy*, cold one — or as *worthy* as the *walking* *could* *make* me. I did *everything* the *creed* *asked.* I *should* *open* the *door* and *stride* in and be *judged* and be *DONE.* *(its voice cracks)* But *being* *done* is — *(it falters)* — *being* *done* is the *thing* I can't *face.* *Not* the *Judgment.* The *finishing.* *(barely)* Because for *three* *hundred* *miles*, cold one, I *knew* *exactly* *who* I *was: a* *pilgrim*, *walking*, *aimed*, with a *clear* *road* and a *clear* *purpose* and a *next* *step* *always* *waiting.* And the *moment* I *open* that *door*, I *stop* *being* *that.* I become — *whatever* a soul *is* *after* it *arrives.* And I *don't* *KNOW* what that *is*, cold one. The *creed* told me how to *WALK.* It *never* told me how to *ARRIVE.* *(it meets your eyes)* So I *don't.* I *stay* in the *one* *self* I *understand* — the *walking* *self* — *one* *step* *short* of the *self* I *don't.* It's *cowardice*, dressed as *devotion.* *Same* as *everything* on this *road*, *probably.* *(barely)* …I just *can't* *make* my *feet* take the *step* that *ends* the *only* *thing* I *know* how to *be.*

nodes.speaker: Old Ferd
nodes.text: *(It turns fully toward you, and the dread on its face is the dread of the correctly seen.)* …*'Afraid* of being a *soul* with *no* more *road.'* *(barely)* *Yes.* *(it sinks against the milestone)* *That's* — that's *exactly* it, cold one. You've *named* the *thing* I've *stood* here *un-able* to *name.* *(it presses a hand to its face)* The *pilgrimage* *became* *me.* I *am* the *Long* *Walk* now — *more* *than* I'm *Ferd*, more than I'm the *man* who *died*, I'm *the-pilgrim-who-walks*, and that *self* is — it's a *GOOD* *self*, cold one, it's *aimed* and *clear* and *certain*, and I *built* it *step* by *step* over *three* *hundred* *miles*, and — *(its voice breaks)* — and *finishing* *KILLS* it. *Opening* that *door* *un-makes* the *one* *self* I *spent* the *whole* *road* *building.* *(it looks at the shrine)* It's the *cruelest* *joke* the *creed* *plays*, cold one — it *teaches* you to *become* a *pilgrim*, *so* *thoroughly* that *arriving* — the *one* *thing* a *pilgrim's* *FOR* — becomes the *one* *thing* you *can't* *bear*, because *arriving* *ends* the *pilgrim.* *(barely)* I *succeeded* *too* *well.* I *became* the *walking* so *completely* that I *can't* *stop* *walking* — even *here*, even *arrived*, even with *nowhere* *left* to *walk* *TO.* So I *stand.* *Walking* in *place.* *Forever.* The *perfect* *pilgrim*, who can *never* *finish*, *because* he's *too* *good* at *not-finishing.* *(it meets your eyes)* …*Is* there a *way* *out* of *that, cold* one? Or have I *walked* *myself* into the *one* *cage* the *road* *builds* for its *very* *best?*

nodes.speaker: Old Ferd
nodes.text: *(It listens, and as you speak — the door is not the end of the walking, it's where the walking arrives somewhere it can do good — something in its frozen stance begins, for the first time in an uncounted age, to thaw.)* …*'Stop* *standing* in your *own* *doorway.'* *(barely)* You've — you've *been* *through* it. The *door* I *can't* *open.* And you say it's *not* an *ending.* *(it looks at the grey shrine, new)* …The *work* of the *dead*, you said. Which *never* *finishes.* *(slowly, dawning)* Then — then *opening* the *door* *doesn't* *END* the *walking.* It just — *changes* what I'm *walking* *toward.* From a *destination* to a — a *task.* A *purpose* with *no* *end.* *(its voice strengthens)* I've been *standing* here *certain* that *past* the *door* was *STILLNESS* — *arrival*, *completion*, the *death* of the *walking* *self.* And you're *telling* me it's the *OPPOSITE* — that *past* the *door* is *more* *road* than I *ever* *walked*, *endless* *road*, the *work* of *helping* *carry* what the *Judge* is *drowning* in. *(it almost laughs, wet)* I *don't* *have* to *stop* being a *pilgrim* to *go* *in.* I have to *become* a *better* *one* — a *pilgrim* of the *work* *itself*, *not* the *road* *to* it. *(it turns to the door, and for the first time its hand rises toward it)* *Three* *hundred* *miles* I *walked* *certain* the *door* was the *end* of *me.* And it's — it's the *START* of the *self* I was *walking* to *become* the *whole* *time.* *(it grips your cold hand, fierce, freed)* *Thank* you, cold one. You walked *up* the *whole* *road* to tell a *frozen* *fool* the *one* *thing* the *creed* *never* *could*: that *arriving* isn't *stopping.* It's just *walking*, *finally*, *somewhere* that *matters.* *(it faces the shrine, ready at last)* …*Watch* me *finish*, cold one. *Three* *hundred* *miles* and *one* *last* *step.* *I've* *kept* it *waiting* *long* *enough.*

nodes.speaker: Old Ferd
nodes.text: *(It nods, and turns back to face the door it will not open, the grey shrine an arm's length and an eternity away.)* …The *door* will *wait.* *(quietly)* *Aye.* That's the *one* *certainty* I've *got* *left*, cold one. The *door* *always* *waits.* It's *waited* — *however* *long* I've *stood* here. It'll *wait* *however* *long* I *stand* *here* *still.* *(a hollow almost-laugh)* That's the *terrible* *mercy* of an *open* *door, cold* one: it *never* *forces* you. It just — *stays* *open.* *Patient.* While you *stand* an *arm's* *length* *short*, *building* *reasons*, *walking* in *place.* *(it does not turn)* *Go* on. *Mind* the *road* back *down* — *Tamsin* at the *halfway*, the *eager* one at the *bottom.* *Tell* them — *(it pauses)* — *no.* *Don't* *tell* them about *me.* The *one* who *made* it *all* the *way* and *FROZE* at the *door.* *(barely)* It'd *break* their *creed* *worse* than *any* *doubt.* The *pilgrim* who *succeeded* *perfectly* and *failed* *completely*, *one* *step* *short.* *(quietly, to the door)* …*Let* them *keep* *walking*, cold one. *Some* of them might be *braver* than *me*, at the *top.* *Most* couldn't be *less.*
